meridian_skill.description:
The meta skill. Turn any raw feature into a properly-skilled, tested,
resolvable unit of agent capability. Cross-modal eval is the recommended
Phase 3 quality gate: 3 frontier models from different providers critique
the output, you iterate to quality, THEN write tests that lock in the
proven-good behavior.
meridian_skill.name: skillify

outline.text: Phase 0: Should This Be a Skill?
Phase 3: Cross-Modal Eval (item 3) — THE QUALITY GATE
Step 2: Run the skill, capture output
Step 4: Cycle until you pass (≤3 cycles)

sections.heading: The Checklist
lines: codeblock:plain:

sections.heading: Phase 0: Should This Be a Skill?
lines: use judgment to follow the Phase 0: Should This Be a Skill? guidance:
Before skillifying, check:
item: Will this be invoked 2+ times? (One-off work ≠ skill)
item: Is there >20 lines of logic? (Trivial helpers don't need full infrastructure)
item: Does it have a clear trigger phrase a user would actually say?
If no to all three, it's a script, not a skill. Move on

sections.heading: Phase 1: Audit
lines: codeblock:plain:RmVhdHVyZTogW25hbWVdCkNvZGU6IFtwYXRoXQpNaXNzaW5nIGl0ZW1zOiBbY2hlY2sgZWFjaCBvZiB0aGUgMTFd

sections.heading: SKILL.md frontmatter template (copy-paste):
lines: codeblock:yaml:
Body must include: **Contract** (what it guarantees), **Phases** (step-by-step), **Output Format** (what it produces)
Extract deterministic code into `scripts/*.ts`

sections.heading: Phase 3: Cross-Modal Eval (item 3) — THE QUALITY GATE
Why this comes before tests
lines: Tests lock in behavior. If the behavior is mediocre, tests lock in mediocrity
Cross-modal eval proves the quality bar FIRST, then tests cement it

sections.heading: Step 1: Pick a representative input
lines: Choose the input that exercises the skill's hardest documented use case. If
unsure: use the primary trigger example from SKILL.md, or the most complex
real-world input from the last 7 days of memory files

sections.heading: Step 2: Run the skill, capture output
lines: Run the skill on the representative input. The OUTPUT FILE is what gets
evaluated

sections.heading: Step 3: Run the eval gate
lines: use judgment to follow the Step 3: Run the eval gate guidance:
codeblock:bash:Z2JyYWluIGV2YWwgY3Jvc3MtbW9kYWwgXAogIC0tdGFzayAiV2hhdCB0aGlzIHNraWxsIGlzIHN1cHBvc2VkIHRvIGFjY29tcGxpc2giIFwKICAtLW91dHB1dCBza2lsbHMvPHNsdWc+L1NLSUxMLm1k
The command runs 3 frontier models from 3 different providers in parallel,
scores the OUTPUT against the TASK on 5 documented dimensions, and writes a
receipt under `~/.gbrain/.gbrain/eval-receipts/<slug>-<sha8>.json` (the
sha-8 binds the receipt to the current SKILL.md content — re-running after
edits writes a new receipt)
**Default models** (override per slot via `--slot-a-model`, `--slot-b-model`,
`--slot-c-model`):

table:decision:fCBTbG90IHwgRGVmYXVsdCB8IFByb3ZpZGVyIHwKfC0tLS0tLXwtLS0tLS0tLS18LS0tLS0tLS0tLXwKfCBBIHwgYG9wZW5haTpncHQtNG9gIHwgT3BlbkFJIHwKfCBCIHwgYGFudGhyb3BpYzpjbGF1ZGUtb3B1cy00LTdgIHwgQW50aHJvcGljIHwKfCBDIHwgYGdvb2dsZTpnZW1pbmktMS41LXByb2AgfCBHb29nbGUgfA==
**These MUST be frontier models from DIFFERENT providers.** Using a single
provider's family or budget models defeats the purpose — different families
have less correlated blind spots. Refresh the list when a new model
generation ships
**Pass criteria (BOTH must be true):**
Every dimension's mean across successful models ≥ 7
No single model scored any dimension < 5 (the floor)
**Inconclusive:** fewer than 2 of 3 models returned parseable scores
Receipt is still written (forensics) but the gate is not authoritative
Exit code 2; CI wrappers should treat this as "did not run cleanly", not
"failed quality gate"

sections.heading: Step 4: Cycle until you pass (≤3 cycles)
lines: codeblock:plain:

sections.heading: Provider configuration
lines: use judgment to follow the Provider configuration guidance:
Models resolve through the gbrain AI gateway. Configure once with:
codeblock:bash:Z2JyYWluIHByb3ZpZGVycyB0ZXN0ICAgICMgc2VlIHdoYXQncyBjb25maWd1cmVkCmdicmFpbiBjb25maWcgICAgICAgICAgICAjIHNldCBrZXlz
Or set env vars: `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`,
`GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY`, `TOGETHER_API_KEY`, etc. The gateway reads
from `~/.gbrain/config.json` plus `process.env`

sections.heading: Cost expectations
lines: use judgment to follow the Cost expectations guidance:
3 cycles × 3 models = 9 frontier calls max per run. With Opus-class +
GPT-4o-class + Gemini-1.5-Pro, expect $1–3 per full run on default
`--max-tokens 4000`. Receipts include the per-call model identifiers so
you can audit retroactively

sections.heading: Phase 4: Tests (items 4-6)
lines: use judgment to follow the Phase 4: Tests (items 4-6) guidance:
NOW that eval has proven quality, write tests that lock it in:
**Unit tests** — every branch of deterministic logic. Mock external calls
**Integration tests** — hit real endpoints. Catch bugs mocks hide
**LLM evals** — quality/correctness for LLM steps. Lighter than cross-modal eval — test specific behaviors

sections.heading: Phase 5: Resolver + Check-Resolvable (items 7-9)
lines: use judgment to follow the Phase 5: Resolver + Check-Resolvable (items 7-9) guidance:
Add to skills/RESOLVER.md with trigger phrases users ACTUALLY type
Resolver eval: feed triggers, assert correct routing
Check-resolvable:
item: Skill reachable from skills/RESOLVER.md (not orphaned)
item: No MECE overlap with other skills
item: No DRY violations (shared logic in lib/, not copy-pasted)
item: No ambiguous trigger routing

sections.heading: Phase 6: E2E + Brain Filing (items 10-11)
lines: use judgment to follow the Phase 6: E2E + Brain Filing (items 10-11) guidance:
item: E2E smoke: full pipeline from trigger to side effect
item: Brain filing: add to brain/RESOLVER.md if the skill writes brain pages

sections.heading: Phase 7: Verify
lines: codeblock:bash:

sections.heading: Worked Example: Skillifying a "summarize-pr" Feature
lines: codeblock:plain:

sections.heading: Quality Gates
lines: NOT properly skilled until:
All required items pass (1-2, 4-10; 11 only when applicable)
Cross-modal eval (item 3) has a current receipt OR is explicitly waived
with rationale (item 3 is informational; not blocking, but a missing
receipt is visible in the audit)
All tests pass (unit + integration + LLM evals)
Resolver entry exists with real trigger phrases
Check-resolvable shows no orphans, overlaps, or DRY violations
Brain filing if applicable

sections.heading: Output Format
lines: Skillify produces three durable artifacts per skill:
**The skill tree on disk.** `skills/<slug>/SKILL.md`, `scripts/<slug>.mjs`,
`routing-eval.jsonl`, plus a `test/<slug>.test.ts` skeleton. Generated by
`gbrain skillify scaffold <name>` and refined by the human/agent into a
real implementation
**A cross-modal eval receipt** at
`~/.gbrain/.gbrain/eval-receipts/<slug>-<sha8>.json`. The sha-8 binds the
receipt to the current `SKILL.md` content. `gbrain skillify check`
surfaces the status (`found` / `stale` / `missing`) as informational
**An audit verdict** from `gbrain skillify check`: `properly skilled` |
`close — create: <missing items>` | `needs skillify — run /skillify on
<target>`. Score is `<passed>/<total>`. Required items gate the verdict;
item 11 (cross-modal eval) is informational and never blocks PASS
JSON output (`gbrain skillify check --json`) includes the same fields plus
the per-item detail string, so agents can route on the structured envelope
without parsing prose

sections.heading: Anti-Patterns
lines: checklist:ai-autonomy:

meridian_skill.triggers:
"skillify this"
"skillify"
"is this a skill?"
"make this proper"
"add tests and evals for this"
"check skill completeness"
